# Task 02.10: Crawl and Freshness Baseline

## Sprint Info
- **Duration:** 4 days
- **Milestone:** M1.5 - Search Quality Evaluation Baseline
- **Priority:** P0
- **Depends On:** Task 02.6
- **Blocks:** Tasks 03.1, 03.5, 08.1, 12.2, 14.1

## What You'll Build
Define the crawl, canonicalization, and freshness signals needed before authority and ranking features can be trusted.

## Daily Breakdown

### Day 1: Crawl Quality Signals
- [ ] Define index coverage, fetch success, parse success, canonical URL coverage, and duplicate URL rate.
- [ ] Define robots, noindex, noarchive, canonical, redirect, and sitemap handling expectations.

### Day 2: Freshness Signals
- [ ] Define crawl timestamp, first-seen timestamp, content-updated timestamp, publication timestamp, and freshness confidence.
- [ ] Define recrawl priority signals for news, commerce, evergreen, and low-quality pages.

### Day 3: Baseline Reporting
- [ ] Create a crawl/freshness dashboard spec.
- [ ] Add stale-result and missing-result failure classes to evaluation reports.
- [ ] Define freshness acceptance criteria for high-freshness query buckets.

### Day 4: Integration Plan
- [ ] Document where freshness and crawl signals enter indexing, feature store, and ranking.
- [ ] Define handoff requirements for M2 link graph, M6 ranking fusion, M9 performance, and M9 security.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Crawl/freshness metrics are defined before link graph work starts.
- [ ] Stale-result checks are part of the query evaluation report.
- [ ] Canonicalization and robots/noindex expectations are documented.
- [ ] Freshness signals have owners and downstream consumers.

## Required Evidence
- Crawl quality metric definitions.
- Freshness feature contract.
- Dashboard/report mock.
- Handoff checklist for M2/M6/M9.
